title: Attack Vector Selection
summary: Choosing approach angles that maximize own firepower while minimizing enemy response.
details: Select attack vectors based on enemy weapon arcs and armor distribution. Most ships have stronger forward weapons and armor - approach from flanks or rear when possible. Consider relative velocity: passing attacks limit engagement time but exposure; head-on attacks are dangerous but bring maximum firepower. Account for enemy escort positions to avoid crossfire. Coordinate with wingmen to attack from multiple vectors simultaneously.
annotations: Intelligence on enemy ship layouts is invaluable.
Rear attacks on capital ships often expose engine weak points.
